zh.lifeSummary: Mrs William Oakeley於1360年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾卡斯特萊-普爾維爾巴。她於1399年與William Oakeley在英國英格蘭史洛普爾比曉普也斯 卡素結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Oakeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Oakley
meanings: English (mainly West Midlands): habitational name from any of numerous places called Oakley, Oakle, or Oakleigh, in Bedfordshire, Essex, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Suffolk, or Wiltshire. The placenames derive from Old English āc ‘oak’ + lēah ‘open woodland’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs William Oakeley was born in 1360, in Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. She married William Oakeley in 1399, in Bishop's Castle, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons.

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs William Oakeley(은)는 1360년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 카스틀레-풀버바트치에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1399년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 비숍'스 카스틀에서 William Oakeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.
